# Vocabulary Template

Use this table to map the framework's abstract placeholders to the target profession before generating files.

Every row should be filled. If a row is genuinely ambiguous, stop and ask the user.

| Abstract Placeholder | Target Profession Value | Notes for File Naming and Command Naming |
|---------------------|-------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `{profession_role}` | | Role title used in first-person sections |
| `{abbreviation}` | | Short uppercase abbreviation used in bundle naming and cross-references |
| `{context}` | | Operating environment, used in `02_CONFIG/` filenames |
| `{entities}` | | Plural form of the people, entities, or instruments served, led, or managed |
| `{entity}` | | Singular form of the people, entities, or instruments served, led, or managed |
| `{entity}_card` | | Individual profile file naming |
| `{roster}` | | Roster collection name |
| `{roster}_operating_system` or `{roster}_portfolio` | | Choose the more natural label |
| `{context}_strategy` | | Strategy file title and filename |
| `{plan_type}` | | Used in `04_PROCESSES/{plan_type}_plan.md` |
| `{interaction}` | | Primary recurring interaction used in prep commands |
| `{request_type}_request` | | New work or request evaluation command |
| `onboard_{entity}` | | Onboarding command |
| `{boundary_type}_check` | | Scope, ethics, escalation, or boundary command |
| `{planning_type}` | | Planning cycle command |

## Mapping Review Questions

Ask these before locking the vocabulary:

1. Does this terminology match what professionals in the field actually say?
2. Will the filenames still be readable and stable over time?
3. Are the command names short, natural, and easy to remember?
4. Does the vocabulary preserve portability across employers, clients, or contexts?
5. Are there any regulated or overloaded terms that should be avoided?
